reviewer: agent matrix pass + off-main-thread composite retrofit (invariant 15), revision 3

AGENT VERDICT: real headless-Chrome run on the cats sample returned 2 per-instance segments with masks — insideRows: '1 cat thing 99.9% 97.5%', '2 remote thing 99.9% 0.9%' — correct panoptic output (per-instance masks + thing/stuff split + area partition). conformance io-shape-semantic=manual now agent-confirmed. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/detr-panoptic/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/detr-panoptic/mobile.png
rubric.notes: Confirmed: pipeline returns [{score,label,mask}]; page composites per-segment masks into one colour-coded overlay (argmax ownership), splits things vs stuff, and lists every segment as accessible text.

No canned output path in source. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/detr-panoptic/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/detr-panoptic/mobile.png

rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): 2✓ 0✗ 0▨ 0◍ — images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/detr-panoptic/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/detr-panoptic/mobile.png

AGENT VERDICT: matrix pass on 1280×800 + 360×740 (DPR3) — responsive-check reported desktop clean · mobile clean (no horizontal overflow, no clipped controls, no sub-24px targets flagged, clean console + network). Read both screenshots: text wraps, table/legend reflow, canvas is inline-size:100%. parity-both-classes=confirmed; support set ok/ok. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/detr-panoptic/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/detr-panoptic/mobile.png
rubric.notes: canvas-grid uses flex-wrap + flex 1 1 300px + min-inline-size:0 (css-layout guidance) so the two panels stack on mobile with no overflow.

rubric.evidence: AGENT VERDICT (invariant-15 retrofit): the dense-output mask→colour-overlay composite now runs OFF the main thread. worker.js builds the colour-coded RGBA overlay in an OffscreenCanvas and transfers an ImageBitmap back (transferToImageBitmap); the page only drawImage()s it and the opacity slider re-drawImage()s the same bitmap — no main-thread putImageData per render/tick. MEASURED, headless Chrome (software raster): real DETR masks return at 512×384, so the pre-existing main-thread render composite was already small (~0.5ms) — the retrofit removes it entirely (worker compositeMs 0.4 off-main; main-thread paint drawImage-only). Synthetic scaling proof (full-res inputs): paintPanoptic composite median 14.5ms→3.2ms @12MP (4032×3024) and 1.7ms→0.0ms @2MP; MessageChannel opacity-drag stall-probe worst gap 36ms→11.4ms @12MP, 16ms→1.4ms @2MP. New immutable conformance assertion worker-compliance added. inp-stays-low still manual.
rubric.notes: The isolate toggle builds its single-segment overlay ONCE per click (cached canvas) instead of per opacity tick. The remaining full-res getImageData class-hit-testing (wild/multi-model per-pixel extract) is a discrete user action, not the continuous render path — tracked as a follow-up, out of this composite-retrofit's scope. createModelLoader auto-init unchanged.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel canvas overlay without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Flexbox with flex:1 1 <basis>, flex-wrap:wrap, min-inline-size:0 on flex items with long/canvas content, logical properties and intrinsic sizing instead of fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .canvas-grid is flex-wrap with .canvas-card {flex:1 1 300px; min-inline-size:0}; canvas is inline-size:100%; block-size:auto; legend/table use flex-wrap. Result: desktop two-up, mobile stacked, no horizontal overflow (verified on 1280×800 + 360×740).

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long tasks INP responsiveness
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy synchronous work off the main thread so input/paint stay responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the full segmentation pass AND the per-pixel mask compositing run in models/detr-panoptic/worker.js (a Web Worker); the worker now also rasterises the colour-coded overlay into an OffscreenCanvas and transfers an ImageBitmap, so the main thread only drawImage()s it (no full-res putImageData). The main thread only paints the returned bitmap + reads the slider.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass + worker-compliance=pass; source uses new Worker(worker.js) + OffscreenCanvas.transferToImageBitmap

guidanceConsulted.query: move heavy canvas pixel compositing off the main thread with a worker and OffscreenCanvas transferable ImageBitmap; keep INP low during opacity slider drag
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: INP & Main-Thread Unblocking — separate UI updates from heavy computations and push background processing to a Web Worker; break up tasks over 50ms; keep the main thread painting only.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the mask→colour overlay composite moved into the inference worker (OffscreenCanvas + transferToImageBitmap → transferred ImageBitmap). The main thread's paintPanoptic is a bare drawImage of the transferred bitmap; the opacity slider re-drawImage()s the cached bitmap rather than re-running putImageData. OffscreenCanvas/transferToImageBitmap/transferable ImageBitmap are Baseline widely-available (same primitives lib/worker-protocol.js documents); a guarded main-thread putImageData fallback (overlayToDrawable) remains for worker realms without OffscreenCanvas.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: MEASURED composite median 14.5ms→3.2ms @12MP, 1.7ms→0.0ms @2MP; MessageChannel opacity-drag stall 36ms→11.4ms @12MP, 16ms→1.4ms @2MP; live headless run: overlayBitmap=ImageBitmap 512×384, worker compositeMs 0.4, console+network clean

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible loading progress and keyboard-operable custom controls
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Semantic live regions for status, labelled keyboard-operable controls, visible focus.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — loader + run status use role=status aria-live; every control is a real <button>/<input> with a label; sample thumbs + dropzone have role=button + Enter/Space handlers + :focus-visible; the legend segments are real toggle buttons with aria-pressed.

guidanceConsulted.query: canvas overlay masks exposed to assistive tech
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Canvas pixels are not exposed to assistive tech / find-in-page; provide an accessible parallel representation (HTML-in-canvas is experimental / non-Baseline, so use a fallback).
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (fallback path) — the result canvas carries a descriptive aria-label AND every segment is ALSO rendered as accessible text: the See-inside table (label · kind · score · area) and the toggle legend. No reliance on the experimental layoutsubtree API.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: page has <canvas aria-label> + #insideRows table + #legend buttons mirroring the canvas

openQuestions: On very busy scenes (dozens of instances) the legend grows long — is a scroll/collapse affordance worth adding on mobile?
Should overlapping thing/stuff boundaries expose a per-pixel confidence, not just argmax ownership?

followUpGoals.goal: Consider a 'download the parsed map' export on the basics page (PNG of the colour-coded overlay) to match the wild page's cut-out export.

summary: Revision-3 for detr-panoptic (panoptic image-segmentation, Xenova/detr-resnet-50-panoptic, wasm/q8). Revision 2's matrix pass (real per-instance masks + thing/stuff split, auto-init-on-cached, mobile+desktop clean) still holds. Revision 3 is an invariant-15 in-place perf retrofit: the dense-output mask→colour-overlay composite moved into the inference worker (OffscreenCanvas → transferToImageBitmap → transferred ImageBitmap); the page is now drawImage-only and the opacity slider re-draws the cached bitmap (no per-tick putImageData). Measured composite 14.5ms→3.2ms @12MP; live headless run returns overlayBitmap=ImageBitmap with clean console/network and identical segments/labels. New immutable conformance assertion worker-compliance added. Route/slug/identity/controls/examples unchanged. Guidance: performance (INP/main-thread unblocking), break-up-long-tasks, plus css-layout, accessibility, expose-canvas-content-to-browser-features. Support ok on both classes.
